nodes.label: In agent-native architecture, features are prompts — not code
nodes.description: The shift from coding specific functions to describing outcomes that agents achieve by composing atomic tools
nodes.source: @danshipper — Agent-Native Architectures (co-authored with Claude)

nodes.label: Files are the universal interface between humans and agents
nodes.description: Markdown and YAML files on disk beat databases because agents already know file operations and humans can inspect everything
nodes.source: @danshipper + @nicbstme — Agent-Native Architectures + Fintool

nodes.label: Technical knowledge can become a liability when working with AI
nodes.description: Experts get stuck on implementation details while novices describe outcomes and ship faster
nodes.source: @WorkflowWhisper (Alton Syn) — tweets on n8n and automation gap closing

nodes.label: Context is the product, not the model
nodes.description: Anyone can call the API — differentiation comes from the data you access, skills you build, UX you design, and domain knowledge you encode
nodes.source: @nicbstme (Nicolas Bustamante) — Lessons from Building AI Agents for Financial Services

nodes.label: Markdown skill files may replace expensive fine-tuning
nodes.description: A SKILL.md file that teaches an agent how to do something specific can match domain-specific fine-tuned models — at zero training cost
nodes.source: Nicolas Bustamante (@nicbstme) — Lessons from Building AI Agents for Financial Services

nodes.label: Deputies and Sheriffs — distributed agent teams with hierarchical authority replace centralized software
nodes.description: Individual employees train specialized 'Deputy' agents while organizational 'Sheriff' agents manage permissions, rules, and onboarding across the team
nodes.source: @danshipper — Personal Software and AI Agents

nodes.label: Data agent failures stem from missing business context, not SQL generation gaps
nodes.description: The industry initially blamed text-to-SQL capability for data agent failures, but the real blockers are undefined business definitions, ambiguous sources of truth, and missing tribal knowledge
nodes.source: @jasonscui — Your Data Agents Need Context, a16z

nodes.label: Decision traces are the missing data layer — a trillion-dollar gap
nodes.description: Systems store what happened but not why; capturing the reasoning behind decisions creates searchable precedent and a new system of record
nodes.source: Jaya Gupta & Ashu Garg — Foundation Capital, Context Graphs

nodes.label: Declarative beats imperative when working with agents
nodes.description: Give agents success criteria and watch them go — don't tell them what to do step by step
nodes.source: Andrej Karpathy — Coding Observations

nodes.label: The UI moat collapses — API quality becomes the purchasing criterion
nodes.description: When agents are the primary users of software, beautiful dashboards stop mattering and API design becomes the competitive surface
nodes.source: @chrysb (Chrys Bader) + @nicbstme (Nicolas Bustamante) — Apps Are Dead + Every SaaS Is Now an API

nodes.label: SaaS survives as the governance and coordination layer — determinism still rules
nodes.description: When non-deterministic AI feeds into deterministic systems (databases, approvals, audit trails), the deterministic system governs; SaaS is that system
nodes.source: @clarashih (Clara Shih) — Head of Business AI at Meta (former CEO of Salesforce AI), How to Survive the SaaS Reckoning

nodes.label: Software abundance unlocks entire categories of applications that never existed
nodes.description: Software has always been more expensive than we can afford; when AI drops costs 10-20x, previously unviable software becomes economically possible
nodes.source: @dabit3 (Nader Dabit) — Joining Cognition / Software Abundance

nodes.label: AI's self-improvement loop means each generation builds the next one faster
nodes.description: GPT-5.3-Codex was instrumental in creating itself — recursive improvement compresses timelines and explains why building for obsolescence is the only safe strategy
nodes.source: @mattshumer_ (Matt Shumer) — Something Big Is Happening

nodes.label: Verification is the single highest-leverage practice for agent-assisted coding
nodes.description: Giving an agent a way to verify its own work 2-3x the quality of output — without verification, you're shipping blind
nodes.source: Boris Cherny + Anthropic Official Best Practices

nodes.label: Verification is a Red Queen race — optimizing against a fixed eval contaminates it
nodes.description: Eval suites degrade the moment you use them to improve an agent — the agent adapts to the distribution, and the eval stops measuring what it was designed to measure
nodes.source: @natashamalpani (Natasha Malpani) — The Verification Economy: The Red Queen Problem (Part III)

nodes.label: Cross-user knowledge transfer works without fine-tuning — just a database and prompt engineering
nodes.description: When one person teaches an agent something, another person benefits automatically — no RLHF, no training infrastructure, just structured storage and retrieval
nodes.source: Ashpreet Bedi — Agents That Learn (Agno Framework)

nodes.label: Boring tech wins for AI-native startups — simpler stack means faster AI-assisted shipping
nodes.description: React + Node + TypeScript + Postgres + Redis scales to $1M ARR with 3 engineers; monorepo is a superpower for AI coding assistants
nodes.source: Kushal Byatnal — Extend ($1M+ ARR, 3 engineers)

nodes.label: Spec files are external memory that survives context resets
nodes.description: A structured specs/ folder (design.md, implementation.md, decisions.md) bridges human intent and agent execution across sessions
nodes.source: Community pattern — spec-first development (implementations by AWS Kiro, GitHub spec-kit, and multiple Claude Code workflows)

nodes.label: Persistent agent memory preserves institutional knowledge that walks out the door with employees
nodes.description: When agents maintain daily changelogs, decision logs, and work preferences, organizational knowledge survives personnel changes
nodes.source: @nicbstme (Nicolas Bustamante) + @rohit4verse (Rohit) — Memory Architecture

nodes.label: Similarity is not relevance — relevance requires reasoning
nodes.description: Vector search finds semantically similar content, but what users need is relevant content, and determining relevance requires LLM reasoning
nodes.source: PageIndex by VectifyAI — https://github.com/VectifyAI/PageIndex

nodes.label: Stronger models expand the verification gap, not close it
nodes.description: More capable models increase the deployment surface and raise the stakes of failures, making verification infrastructure more valuable rather than less
nodes.source: @natashamalpani (Natasha Malpani) — The Verification Economy: The Red Queen Problem (Part III)

nodes.label: Structure plus reasoning beats flat similarity for complex domains
nodes.description: Across documents, code, and skills, the same pattern holds: structured knowledge navigated by reasoning outperforms flat indexes searched by similarity
nodes.source: Recurring pattern across PageIndex, Claude Code agentic search, and @arscontexta skill graphs

nodes.label: Agentic search beats RAG for live codebases
nodes.description: Claude Code abandoned RAG and vector DB in favor of letting the agent grep/glob/read — reasoning about where to look outperforms pre-indexed similarity search
nodes.source: Boris Cherny (@bcherny, Claude Code team) — Twitter reply to @EthanLipnik

nodes.label: Personal software grows through relationship, not configuration
nodes.description: Unlike traditional SaaS where users adapt to the tool, personal software agents grow personality and skills in response to their user through ongoing interaction
nodes.source: @danshipper — Personal Software and AI Agents

nodes.label: Platform economics beat labor arbitrage — margins fund flywheels that body shops cannot
nodes.description: Scale AI's 50%+ gross margins fund ML pre-labeling and workflow optimization, creating a flywheel; Indian BPOs at 10-15% margins cannot invest in R&D and remain trapped competing on price
nodes.source: @GowriShankarNag — #MarketMapMondays: The Label Paradox of AI, Antler India

nodes.label: PostgreSQL scales further than you think
nodes.description: OpenAI runs ChatGPT on one PostgreSQL primary plus ~50 read replicas handling millions of QPS — no sharding of PostgreSQL itself, just excellent operations
nodes.source: OpenAI — https://openai.com/index/scaling-postgresql/

nodes.label: Response UX should match retrieval intelligence
nodes.description: If your system uses semantic search to find results, the display should reflect that intelligence — keyword highlighting on semantic results creates a confusing mismatch
nodes.source: @akshay_pachaar — 'Your RAG System Has a Hidden UX Problem' (Daily Dose of Data Science blog), referencing Zilliz semantic highlighting model

nodes.label: Skill graphs enable progressive disclosure for complex domains
nodes.description: Single skill files hit a ceiling — complex domains need interconnected knowledge that agents navigate progressively from index to description to links to sections to full content
nodes.source: @arscontexta (Heinrich) — Twitter thread on skill graphs

nodes.label: A skill's folder structure is its context architecture — the file system is a form of context engineering
nodes.description: Skills are not just markdown files but folders where scripts, references, and assets enable progressive disclosure — the agent reads deeper files only when it reaches the relevant step
nodes.source: @trq212 (Thariq) — Lessons from Building Claude Code: How We Use Skills

nodes.label: An orchestrator agent that manages other agents solves the parallel coordination problem without human bottleneck
nodes.description: Instead of humans managing AI agents, a meta-agent spawns specialized agents, routes tasks by model strength, and monitors progress
nodes.source: @elvissun (Elvis Sun) — OpenClaw Agent Swarm

nodes.label: Already-outsourced tasks are the autopilot wedge — vendor swap beats reorg
nodes.description: If work is already outsourced, budget exists, external delivery is accepted, and the buyer purchases outcomes — substitution is frictionless
nodes.source: @julienbek — Services: The New Software

nodes.label: Open source captures value through services, not software
nodes.description: Free software builds billion-dollar companies because the money is in support, cloud, and governance layers — not the code itself
nodes.source: Learning Technical Concepts chat — Ayush exploring open source revenue

nodes.label: WebMCP turns websites into agent-native interfaces
nodes.description: Chrome's MCP integration lets websites expose structured tools to agents instead of agents scraping and guessing at UI elements
nodes.source: Learning Technical Concepts chat — Chrome for Developers WebMCP announcement

nodes.label: Prompt caching makes long context economically viable
nodes.description: Prefix-matching cache enables 80%+ cost reduction for multi-turn conversations, making rich context systems affordable at scale
nodes.source: Learning Technical Concepts chat — Thariq's prompt caching article

nodes.label: Parallel agents create a management problem, not a coding problem
nodes.description: When AI agents can work on multiple projects simultaneously, the bottleneck shifts from writing code to coordinating parallel workstreams
nodes.source: Learning Technical Concepts chat — Theo's tweet about multiple projects with agents

nodes.label: Don't be the discriminator — be the patron, not the judge
nodes.description: Taste (selecting from AI output) is the function that gets automated first; participating in creation through friction and will is what endures
nodes.source: @WillManidis (Will Manidis) — Against Taste

nodes.label: Autonomous coding loops need small stories and fast feedback to work
nodes.description: The Ralph pattern ships 13 features in 1 hour by decomposing into context-window-sized tasks with explicit acceptance criteria and test-based feedback
nodes.source: Ryan Carson — Ralph / Autonomous Coding Loop

nodes.label: Autopilots capture the work budget — six dollars in services for every one in software
nodes.description: Copilots sell tools to professionals; autopilots sell outcomes to end customers and access the vastly larger services TAM from day one
nodes.source: @julienbek — Services: The New Software

nodes.label: AI automation amplifies demand for expert human judgment rather than replacing it
nodes.description: Pre-labeling cuts costs 100,000x for simple tasks, but projects that needed 500 contributors now need 100 doing far higher-value work at up to $200/hour
nodes.source: @GowriShankarNag — #MarketMapMondays: The Label Paradox of AI, Antler India

nodes.label: Technology transitions create more of the 'dying' thing, not less
nodes.description: Every predicted death — mainframes, physical retail, traditional media — resulted in growth of both old and new; AI will create more software, not less
nodes.source: @stevesi (Steven Sinofsky) — Death of Software?

nodes.label: Hybrid search is the default, not the exception
nodes.description: Neither keyword nor semantic search alone is complete — combining BM25 and vector search with reranking is the baseline for production systems
nodes.source: QMD by Tobi Lütke, pg_textsearch by Timescale, TigerData BM25 article

nodes.label: Multi-model code review creates adversarial robustness — each model catches what others miss
nodes.description: Using 3 different LLMs to review the same PR exploits the fact that models have different failure modes, creating emergent coverage no single model achieves
nodes.source: @elvissun (Elvis Sun) — OpenClaw Agent Swarm

nodes.label: Agent trust transfers from human credibility — colleagues adopt agents operated by people they trust
nodes.description: When a human's agent consistently performs well, other team members inherit that trust and willingly depend on the agent, creating a credibility chain
nodes.source: @danshipper — Personal Software and AI Agents

nodes.label: B2B becomes B2A — agents become the buyer
nodes.description: Software is increasingly consumed by agents, not humans; the agent recommends, the human approves
nodes.source: @nicbstme (Nicolas Bustamante) — Every SaaS Is Now an API

nodes.label: Treat AI like a distributed team, not a single assistant
nodes.description: Running 15 parallel Claude streams with specialized roles produces better results than one perfect conversation
nodes.source: Boris Cherny — Creator of Claude Code

nodes.label: Evolving summaries beat append-only memory — rewrite profiles, don't accumulate facts
nodes.description: An evolve_summary() function that rewrites category profiles with new information handles contradictions naturally, unlike append-only logs
nodes.source: Rohit (@rohit4verse) — How to Build Agents That Never Forget

nodes.label: Every optimization has a shadow regression — guard commands make the shadow visible
nodes.description: When optimizing metric A, metric B silently degrades unless you run a separate invariant check (a guard) alongside the primary verification
nodes.source: Udit Goenka (@uditg) — autoresearch Claude Code skill v1.6.1 (Guard feature by Roman Pronskiy, JetBrains)

nodes.label: Treat an agent as an operating system, not a stateless function
nodes.description: Agents need RAM (conversation context), a hard drive (persistent memory), garbage collection (decay/pruning), and I/O management (tools)
nodes.source: Rohit (@rohit4verse) — How to Build Agents That Never Forget

nodes.label: Tiered retrieval prevents context overload — summaries first, details on demand
nodes.description: Reading category summaries first, then drilling to items, then raw resources only if needed keeps memory retrieval within token budgets
nodes.source: Rohit (@rohit4verse) — How to Build Agents That Never Forget

nodes.label: Trust boundaries must be externalized — not held in engineers' heads
nodes.description: Where an agent's behavior is well-understood vs. unknown should be mapped, made auditable, and connected to deployment gates — not left as implicit tribal knowledge
nodes.source: @natashamalpani (Natasha Malpani) — The Verification Economy: The Red Queen Problem (Part III)

nodes.label: Tribal knowledge is the irreducible human input that enables agent automation
nodes.description: Automated context construction handles most of the corpus, but the most critical context is implicit, conditional, and historically contingent — only humans can provide it
nodes.source: @jasonscui — Your Data Agents Need Context, a16z

nodes.label: Session capture turns ephemeral AI conversations into a compounding knowledge base
nodes.description: shadcn's /done pattern — dumping key decisions to markdown after each session — applies file-based memory architecture to development workflow
nodes.source: shadcn (via X/Twitter) — /done skill pattern

nodes.label: Embeddings measure similarity, not truth — vector databases have a temporal blind spot
nodes.description: Vector search can't resolve contradictions or understand time; 'I love my job' and 'I'm quitting' retrieve with equal confidence
nodes.source: Rohit (@rohit4verse) — How to Build Agents That Never Forget

nodes.label: The context window is the fundamental constraint — everything else follows
nodes.description: Every best practice in AI coding (subagents, /clear, focused tasks, specs files) traces back to managing a single scarce resource: context
nodes.source: Anthropic Official Best Practices + Jarrod Watts

nodes.label: The three-layer AI stack: Memory, Search, Reasoning
nodes.description: The emerging AI product architecture has three layers — Memory, Search, Reasoning — all running on PostgreSQL
nodes.source: Synthesis from Supermemory, QMD, and PageIndex architectures

nodes.label: Compound engineering makes each unit of work improve all future work
nodes.description: The 80/20 ratio (80% plan+review, 20% work+compound) ensures learning compounds across iterations, not just code
nodes.source: Dan Shipper & Kieran Klaassen (Every) — Compound Engineering

nodes.label: Commodity work's terminal value is zero but structured expert judgment compounds indefinitely
nodes.description: Appen collapsed from $4.5B to $140M as LLMs displaced commodity annotation, while Scale AI reached $29B by owning expert alignment infrastructure — the market is bifurcating
nodes.source: @GowriShankarNag — #MarketMapMondays: The Label Paradox of AI, Antler India

nodes.label: Building real projects teaches AI skills faster than following structured curricula
nodes.description: A non-technical user who built a production WhatsApp bot reached 'Operator' level that a 30-day AI mastery roadmap targets — through building, not studying
nodes.source: Analysis of Machina (@EXM7777) — 30-Day AI Mastery Roadmap

nodes.label: Production agents route routine cases through decision trees, reserving humans for complexity
nodes.description: Handle exact matches and known patterns without AI; invoke the model for ambiguity, and route genuinely complex cases to human judgment
nodes.source: @vasuman — AI Agents 101

nodes.label: Malleable software — a tiny core that writes its own plugins — replaces fixed-feature applications
nodes.description: Instead of adapting your workflow to the tool, the tool observes your workflow and extends itself to match it
nodes.source: @tolobi (Tobi Lutke, Shopify CEO) — Pi and Clawdbot

nodes.label: Agents that store error patterns learn continuously without fine-tuning or retraining
nodes.description: Dash's 'GPU-poor continuous learning' separates validated knowledge from error-driven learnings — five lines of code replaces expensive retraining
nodes.source: @ashpreetbedi — Dash (OpenAI-inspired data agent)

nodes.label: Tools are a new kind of software — contracts between deterministic systems and non-deterministic agents
nodes.description: Agent tools must be designed for how agents think (context-limited, non-deterministic, description-dependent), not how programmers think
nodes.source: Anthropic Engineering — Writing Effective Tools for Agents

nodes.label: Evaluate agent tools with real multi-step tasks, not toy single-call examples
nodes.description: Weak evaluation tasks hide tool design flaws — strong tasks require chained calls, ambiguity resolution, and verifiable outcomes
nodes.source: Anthropic Engineering — Writing Effective Tools for Agents

nodes.label: Agents eat your system of record — the rigid app was the constraint, not the schema
nodes.description: When agents can clone your entire CRM in seconds and become the real interface, the SaaS product becomes a dumb write endpoint. Data moats evaporate because agents eliminate the rigid app that demanded rigid schemas.
nodes.source: @zain_hoda (Zain Hoda, Vanna AI) — The Agent Will Eat Your System of Record

nodes.label: Rollback safety nets enable autonomous iteration — not model intelligence
nodes.description: The minimum viable safety net for autonomy is a quantifiable metric, atomic changes, and automatic rollback — these make cheap failure possible, which makes aggressive exploration safe
nodes.source: Manthan Gupta (@manthanguptaa) — How Karpathy's Autoresearch Works; Andrej Karpathy — autoresearch program.md

nodes.label: Tool design is continuous observation — see like an agent
nodes.description: Designing effective agent tools requires iterating by watching actual model behavior, not specifying upfront; tools that helped weaker models may constrain stronger ones
nodes.source: @trq212 (Thariq, Claude Code team) — Lessons from Building Claude Code

nodes.label: Domain-specific skill libraries are the real agent moat, not core infrastructure
nodes.description: An elite team can replicate any agent's tool architecture in months, but accumulated domain workflows (LBO modeling, compliance, bankruptcy) represent years of domain expertise
nodes.source: @nicbstme — Lessons from Reverse Engineering Excel AI Agents

nodes.label: Context inefficiency compounds three penalties: cost, latency, and quality degradation
nodes.description: Every wasted token in an LLM context window doesn't just cost money — it slows responses and degrades output quality, creating a triple tax on production agents
nodes.source: @nicbstme — The LLM Context Tax: Best Tips for Tax Avoidance

nodes.label: Context layers must be living systems, not static artifacts
nodes.description: Unlike semantic layers that rot when maintainers leave, context layers need self-updating feedback loops where agent errors refine the context corpus
nodes.source: @jasonscui — Your Data Agents Need Context, a16z

nodes.label: Context layers supersede semantic layers for agent autonomy
nodes.description: Traditional semantic layers handle metric definitions but agents need a superset: canonical entities, identity resolution, tribal knowledge instructions, and governance guidance
nodes.source: @jasonscui — Your Data Agents Need Context, a16z

nodes.label: KV cache hit rate is the most critical metric for production agents
nodes.description: Maintaining stable prompt prefixes and append-only context architecture maximizes cache reuse, dramatically reducing both cost and latency for agentic workflows
nodes.source: @nicbstme — The LLM Context Tax: Best Tips for Tax Avoidance

nodes.label: Lakebases decouple compute from storage — databases become elastic infrastructure
nodes.description: Third-generation databases separate compute and storage entirely, putting data in open formats on cloud object stores; the database becomes a serverless layer that scales to zero
nodes.source: Databricks (Ali Ghodsi, Matei Zaharia et al.) — What Is a Lakebase

nodes.label: LLMs complete Aggregation Theory by collapsing the interface layer
nodes.description: Ben Thompson's framework reaches its final chapter: LLMs eliminate the interface layer that protected software suppliers, turning the entire web into a backend database where suppliers compete on data quality alone
nodes.source: @nicbstme — The Crumbling Workflow Moat: Aggregation Theory's Final Chapter

nodes.label: LLMs selectively destroy vertical software moats — 5 fall, 5 hold
nodes.description: Learned interfaces, custom workflows, public data access, talent scarcity, and bundling collapse under LLMs, while proprietary data, regulatory lock-in, network effects, transaction embedding, and system-of-record status remain defensible
nodes.source: @nicbstme — 10 Years Building Vertical Software: My Perspective on the Selloff

nodes.label: LLM competition fragments markets from 3 incumbents to 300
nodes.description: When LLMs lower the cost of building vertical software, competition doesn't add one new entrant — it explodes combinatorially, explaining market repricing before revenue loss
nodes.source: @nicbstme — 10 Years Building Vertical Software: My Perspective on the Selloff

nodes.label: Safety enforcement belongs in tool design, not system prompts
nodes.description: At scale, embedding safety constraints in the tool's API (blocking destructive operations by default) beats relying on behavioral compliance with system prompt instructions
nodes.source: @nicbstme — Lessons from Reverse Engineering Excel AI Agents

nodes.label: AI won't destroy SaaS moats — it'll make the biggest ones even bigger
nodes.description: Enterprise SaaS consolidates rather than fragments: we could see 5-10 individual trillion-dollar SaaS companies. Moats are people, relationships, and enterprise integrations — not code. Cheaper AI-built software doesn't overcome distribution advantages.
nodes.source: @Konstantine (Konstantine Buhler, Sequoia Capital) — The Great SaaS Consolidation + AI Ascent 2025

nodes.label: Metadata consumed by LLMs needs trigger specifications, not human summaries
nodes.description: When an LLM scans metadata to decide what to invoke, the description should specify when to activate — not summarize what the thing does — because LLMs are a fundamentally different consumer than humans
nodes.source: @trq212 (Thariq) — Lessons from Building Claude Code: How We Use Skills

nodes.label: Model-market fit comes before product-market fit — without it, no amount of product excellence drives adoption
nodes.description: AI startups need a prerequisite layer beneath PMF: the capability threshold where models can actually satisfy market demands. Legal AI crossed it at 87% accuracy; finance AI at 56% hasn't — same demand, opposite outcomes.
nodes.source: @nicbstme (Nicolas Bustamante) — Model-Market Fit

nodes.label: Harness engineering — humans steer, agents execute, documentation is the system of record
nodes.description: OpenAI built a million-line production codebase with zero manually-written code in 5 months. The discipline shifted from writing code to designing the harness: architecture constraints, documentation, tooling, and feedback loops that make agents reliable at scale.
nodes.source: OpenAI Codex Team — Harness Engineering: Leveraging Codex in an Agent-First World

nodes.label: A mediocre agent inside a strong harness outperforms a stronger agent inside a messy one
nodes.description: The surrounding machinery — metrics, rollback, scoping, observability — determines autonomous system performance more than model capability
nodes.source: Manthan Gupta (@manthanguptaa) — How Karpathy's Autoresearch Works And What You Can Learn From It

nodes.label: AI is the computer — orchestration across 19 models is the product, not any single model
nodes.description: Perplexity launched a unified agent system orchestrating 19 backend models that delegate tasks, manage files, execute code, and browse the web. The differentiation isn't the models — it's the orchestration. 'The computer is the orchestration system.'
nodes.source: @AravSrinivas (Aravind Srinivas, Perplexity CEO) — AI Is the Computer

nodes.label: Incentive-caused bias makes good people rationalize harmful behavior
nodes.description: People don't consciously choose to be unethical — incentive structures cause them to drift into immoral behavior and then rationalize it as virtuous
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 11: The Psychology of Human Misjudgment (pp. 505-514)

nodes.label: The intelligence-to-judgement ratio determines which professions AI automates first
nodes.description: Intelligence work (complex but rule-based) is already automatable; judgement (experience, taste, intuition) remains human — software engineering crossed the threshold first
nodes.source: @julienbek — Services: The New Software

nodes.label: Systems that prevent bad behavior beat moral appeals — design the cash register, not the sermon
nodes.description: People who create mechanisms making dishonest behavior hard to accomplish are more effective than those who preach against dishonesty
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 11: The Psychology of Human Misjudgment (pp. 500-511)

nodes.label: First conclusions become nearly permanent — the brain resists its own updates
nodes.description: Inconsistency-Avoidance Tendency means early-formed habits and first conclusions are maintained even against strong disconfirming evidence
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 11: The Psychology of Human Misjudgment (pp. 523-527)

nodes.label: Small concessions trigger disproportionate reciprocation — even at the subconscious level
nodes.description: Reciprocation Tendency operates below conscious awareness, making tiny favors or concessions produce outsized compliance — the only reliable defense is structural prohibition
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 11: The Psychology of Human Misjudgment (pp. 537-545)

nodes.label: Confluence of tendencies produces extreme outcomes — lollapalooza effects emerge when multiple psychological biases push the same direction
nodes.description: When several psychological tendencies combine toward the same outcome, the result is not additive but explosive — Munger's checklist method diagnoses these compound failures
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 11: The Psychology of Human Misjudgment (pp. 599-604)

nodes.label: Social proof makes groups passive before visible harm — conformity overrides individual judgment even in life-or-death situations
nodes.description: Social-Proof Tendency causes individuals to follow the crowd into inaction or corruption, with bystander apathy and institutional silence as its most dangerous manifestations
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 11: The Psychology of Human Misjudgment (pp. 572-579)

nodes.label: Excessive self-regard makes fixable failures persist — people excuse poor performance instead of correcting it
nodes.description: The Tolstoy effect causes people to rationalize fixable shortcomings rather than address them, requiring meritocratic culture and objective evaluation as antidotes
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 11: The Psychology of Human Misjudgment (pp. 556-563)

nodes.label: A latticework of mental models beats isolated facts for real understanding
nodes.description: You can't know anything useful by remembering isolated facts — they must hang on a latticework of theory from multiple disciplines, with 80-90 key models carrying 90% of the freight
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 2: Elementary Worldly Wisdom (pp. 164-170)

nodes.label: Scale advantages cascade toward dominance until bureaucracy kills them
nodes.description: Advantages of scale — cost curves, social proof, informational edge, advertising reach — compound toward winner-take-all, but large organizations breed bureaucracy and territoriality that can undo every advantage
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 2: Elementary Worldly Wisdom (pp. 174-192)

nodes.label: Technology helps moat businesses but kills commodity businesses
nodes.description: In commodity businesses, productivity improvements flow entirely to customers; in businesses with competitive advantages, the same improvements go to the bottom line
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 2: Elementary Worldly Wisdom (pp. 192-198)

nodes.label: Circle of competence determines where you can win
nodes.description: Every person has a circle of competence — playing inside it with discipline compounds advantage, playing outside it guarantees loss, and it's very hard to enlarge
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 2: Elementary Worldly Wisdom (pp. 196-200)

nodes.label: Bet seldom but heavily when the odds are extreme
nodes.description: The wise ones bet big when they have the odds and don't bet the rest of the time — most of Berkshire's billions came from about ten insights over a lifetime
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 2: Elementary Worldly Wisdom (pp. 206-220)

nodes.label: Inference capability lowers input fidelity requirements — smart listeners make imprecise input work
nodes.description: When the consumer of input has strong inference ability, the quality bar for that input drops — voice works not because transcription improved, but because the listener got smarter
nodes.source: @mvanhorn (Matt Van Horn) — Every Claude Code Hack I Know (March 2026)

nodes.label: Invert, always invert — many problems are best solved backward
nodes.description: Thinking in reverse is one of the most powerful problem-solving techniques: instead of asking what you want, ask what you want to avoid, then don't do that
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 4: Practical Thought About Practical Thought (pp. 299-305)

nodes.label: Pavlovian association builds durable brand moats that compound for over a century
nodes.description: Brands are conditioned reflexes — the trade name is the stimulus, purchase is the response, and Pavlovian association with things consumers admire creates advantages that scale economics alone cannot explain
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 4: Practical Thought About Practical Thought (pp. 305-315)

nodes.label: The pilot training model builds reliable knowledge — fluency, checklists, and maintenance prevent cognitive failure
nodes.description: Just as pilot training uses six elements to prevent fatal errors, the same structure should govern all serious professional education
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 5: The Need for More Multidisciplinary Skills (pp. 327-336)

nodes.label: Ideology is among the most extreme distorters of human cognition
nodes.description: Heavy ideology locks your brain into dysfunctional patterns — if it can warp a genius like Chomsky, imagine what it does to ordinary minds
nodes.source: Charlie Munger — Poor Charlie's Almanack, Talk 3: Elementary Worldly Wisdom, Revisited (pp. 235-239)

nodes.label: Weaponize sycophancy with adversarial agent ensembles instead of fighting it
nodes.description: Deploy bug-finder, adversary, and referee agents with scoring incentives that exploit each agent's eagerness to please — triangulating truth from competing biases
nodes.source: @systematicls — How To Be A World-Class Agentic Engineer

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md should be a routing table, not a knowledge base
nodes.description: Structure CLAUDE.md as a minimal IF-ELSE conditional directory that points to context files, not a monolithic knowledge dump that wastes context window
nodes.source: @systematicls — How To Be A World-Class Agentic Engineer

nodes.label: Frontier companies absorb every useful agentic pattern into their products
nodes.description: If a workaround truly works, OpenAI/Anthropic will build it in — skills, memory, planning all get absorbed, making the wrapper layer fragile
nodes.source: @systematicls — How To Be A World-Class Agentic Engineer

nodes.label: One session per contract beats long-running agent sessions
nodes.description: Fresh context per task contract outperforms 24-hour agent sessions because cross-contract context bloat degrades performance by construction
nodes.source: @systematicls — How To Be A World-Class Agentic Engineer

nodes.label: Separate research from implementation to preserve context quality
nodes.description: Research pollutes implementation context with irrelevant exploration tokens — split into separate agent sessions with specs as the handoff mechanism
nodes.source: @systematicls — How To Be A World-Class Agentic Engineer

nodes.label: Sell the work, not the tool — model improvements compound for services, against software
nodes.description: If you sell the tool, you race the model; if you sell the outcome, every model improvement makes your service faster, cheaper, and harder to compete with
nodes.source: @julienbek — Services: The New Software

nodes.label: Observability is the missing discipline for agent systems — you can't improve what you can't measure
nodes.description: Agent systems need telemetry (token usage, latency, error rates, cost per task) as a first-class engineering concern, not an afterthought bolted on after production failures
nodes.source: Geoff Huntley — Latent Patterns Principles (verification over testing)

nodes.label: Property-based testing explores agent input spaces that example-based tests miss
nodes.description: Generative tests that produce random or adversarial inputs discover edge cases in agent behavior that hand-written examples never cover
nodes.source: Geoff Huntley — Latent Patterns Principles (verification over testing)

nodes.label: Proprietary feedback loops create moats that widen with every interaction
nodes.description: When usage generates data that competitors cannot replicate — correction patterns, preference signals, domain-specific edge cases — the product improves faster than any new entrant can catch up
nodes.source: Nikunj Kothari — Revealed Preferences

nodes.label: The 80/99 gap is where AI products die — demo accuracy and production reliability are infinitely far apart
nodes.description: Getting an AI system from 80% demo accuracy to 99% production reliability requires fundamentally different engineering than the first 80%
nodes.source: @nicbstme (Nicolas Bustamante) — Model-Market Fit

nodes.label: Time-bounded evaluation forces optimization for real-world usefulness instead of idealized performance
nodes.description: A fixed wall-clock budget per experiment makes results comparable, normalizes across hardware, and forces agents to optimize for improvement per unit time
nodes.source: Manthan Gupta (@manthanguptaa) — How Karpathy's Autoresearch Works And What You Can Learn From It

nodes.label: Revealed preferences trump stated preferences — track what users do, not what they say
nodes.description: Users' actual behavior (what they click, skip, edit, redo) is the ground truth for product decisions; stated preferences systematically mislead
nodes.source: Nikunj Kothari — Revealed Preferences

nodes.label: Adversarial branch-walking beats review for planning — walk every design branch until resolved
nodes.description: The most effective planning intervention is not post-hoc review or divergent brainstorming but convergent, exhaustive questioning that traverses each branch of the decision tree with recommended answers
nodes.source: @mattpocockuk (Matt Pocock) — grill-me skill (mattpocock/skills, 9.5K stars, 151K views)

nodes.label: Detect everything, notify selectively — the observability-to-notification ratio determines system trust
nodes.description: Watch every signal but ensure alerts reaching humans always mean something; teams ignore noisy monitors AND noisy agents equally fast
nodes.source: @RampLabs — How We Made Ramp Sheets Self-Maintaining

nodes.label: Auto-generated narrow monitors beat handwritten broad checks — a tight mesh over the exact shape of the code
nodes.description: 1,000+ AI-generated monitors that each target specific code paths catch more bugs than 10 hand-written checks that cover general categories
nodes.source: @RampLabs — How We Made Ramp Sheets Self-Maintaining

nodes.label: Unfocused agents develop path dependency — without a specific mission, they explore the same paths repeatedly
nodes.description: Agents given broad mandates converge on familiar exploration paths, catching high-radius issues but missing narrow situational problems
nodes.source: @RampLabs — How We Made Ramp Sheets Self-Maintaining

nodes.label: Reasoning evaporation permanently destroys agent decision chains when the context window closes
nodes.description: An agent's multi-step reasoning exists only in the context window; when the session ends, the output survives but the decision chain — why each step was taken — is gone forever
nodes.source: @rohit4verse (Rohit) — The Missing Layer in Your Agentic Stack

nodes.label: Accumulated agent traces produce emergent world models — discovered, not designed
nodes.description: When agent decision trajectories accumulate over time, they form a context graph that reveals entities, relationships, and constraints nobody explicitly modeled
nodes.source: @rohit4verse (Rohit) — The Missing Layer in Your Agentic Stack

nodes.label: The comfortable middle is over — software companies must either accelerate AI growth or rebuild for 40%+ margins
nodes.description: Growth-path companies ship AI-native products in 4-person pods with token-based pricing; margin-path companies flatten management, raise prices, and let low-value customers churn
nodes.source: @DavidGeorge83 (David George, a16z) — There Are Only Two Paths Left for Software

nodes.label: Cap headcount, not compute — token spend per engineer replaces headcount as the scaling unit
nodes.description: At $1,000/month per engineer as table stakes, top engineers manage 20-30 agents simultaneously; R&D scales through compute investment, not hiring
nodes.source: @DavidGeorge83 (David George, a16z) — There Are Only Two Paths Left for Software
